metadata.title: Microsoft <-> OpenAI relationship
metadata.provenance: Compiled from primary sources (Microsoft blog, OpenAI blog, SEC 8-K ex99 accession 000119312525256310, secondary accounting analyses) plus knowledge fills flagged at med confidence.

entities.name: OpenAI Group PBC
entities.notes: Recapitalized to a public-benefit corp in Oct 2025; SoftBank-led round reportedly ~$500B valuation; revenue run-rate ~$13-20B vs ~$1.4T disclosed compute commitments.

entities.name: Microsoft
entities.notes: Holds ~27% of OpenAI Group; equity-method investee. FY25 P&E capex $64.55B.

edges.notes: ~27% stake in OpenAI Group PBC, reported value ~$135B. Definitive agreement Oct 28 2025.

Cumulative ~$13B (from ~$1B in 2019). A LARGE share was delivered as AZURE CREDITS, not cash - i.e. OpenAI 'spends' the investment back into Microsoft's own cloud. The cleanest round-trip in the whole web: Microsoft books OpenAI's Azure consumption as revenue while having funded part of it. Exact cash/credit split not publicly itemized.

OpenAI's INCREMENTAL $250B Azure purchase commitment. OpenAI commits a quarter-trillion to the cloud of the company that owns 27% of it. Money OpenAI raises (from SoftBank, Nvidia, etc.) routes to Azure, which buys Nvidia GPUs.
Reciprocal revenue-sharing; reported cap around ~$38B over the term; IP rights for Microsoft through ~2032; Azure right-of-first-refusal removed (OpenAI now free to use Oracle/CoreWeave/Google etc.).

OpenAI's NET LOSS flows onto MSFT's income statement via equity method: $3.1B / $0.41 EPS hit in Q1 FY26, up 6x from $523M / $0.07 a year earlier. OpenAI's cash burn is now a visible, growing drag on Microsoft GAAP earnings.

successor_deal_note: An Apr 27 2026 'next phase of the Microsoft-OpenAI partnership' blog post supersedes the Oct 2025 terms (https://blogs.microsoft.com/blog/2026/04/27/the-next-phase-of-the-microsoft-openai-partnership/). Current-status terms should be read against that update; the equity stake and Azure-commitment architecture persist.
